content.problemStatement:
There are NNN players participating in a knockout tournament. The iii-th player has the rating AiA_iAi​. No two players have the same rating.

N−1N-1N−1 matches are played in the tournament. In each match, 222 random players are chosen out of the remaining players, and the player with the higher rating wins. The losing player gets eliminated from the tournament.

Can you find out the expected rating of the player ending up at the second position (that is, the expected rating of the player losing in the (N−1)(N-1)(N−1)-th match)?

The expected rating of the second player can be represented as a fraction of the form PQ\frac{P}{Q}QP​. You are required to print P⋅Q−1(mod1 000 000 007)P \cdot Q^{-1} \pmod{1 \, 000 \, 000 \, 007}P⋅Q−1(mod1000000007).


Input Format

The first line contains TTT - the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
The first line of each test case contains NNN - the number of players participating in the tournament.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,⋯ ,ANA_1, A_2, \cdots , A_NA1​,A2​,⋯,AN​ - the ratings of the players.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a single line the expected rating of the second player modulo 1 000 000 0071 \, 000 \, 000 \, 0071000000007.
constraints: Ai≠AjA_i \ne A_jAi​=Aj​ for all 1≤i<j≤N1 \le i \lt j \le N1≤i<j≤N
The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 5⋅1055 \cdot 10^55⋅105

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 111: There is only one possible match, and the player with rating =1= 1=1 will be the second player.


Test Case 222: The player with rating =1= 1=1 will be the second player with probability 13\frac{1}{3}31​. The player with rating =2= 2=2 will be the second player with probability 23\frac{2}{3}32​. Therefore, the expected rating of the second player is 1⋅13+2⋅23=531 \cdot \frac{1}{3} + 2 \cdot \frac{2}{3} = \frac{5}{3}1⋅31​+2⋅32​=35​.
